content: pg_restore --clean drops existing objects before recreating them. --if-exists adds IF EXISTS to DROP statements preventing errors on a fresh database. pg_restore does NOT automatically terminate active connections; active connections will block the restore if they hold locks. For Docker Compose restores, stopping the coder service first (docker compose stop coder) removes its connections, leaving only the pg_restore session itself. Alternatively, SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname='coder' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid() terminates all non-self connections. Input is read from stdin using shell redirection (< dump.file) when used with docker compose exec -T.
